---
title: Looking at the sex of the cells
date: 2024-07-05
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Determine sex breakdown of UBC cells
2. Determine sex breakdown of RL cells (human only, mouse only, together)

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240705`

# Process and Results

Using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(patchwork)
```

Loading in the Seurat object
```{r eval=FALSE}
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240705"
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
```

Subsetting the human UBCs
```{r eval=FALSE}
sexes <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/dataset_sexes.csv")
# First subset human cells, then subset for UBC cells, then Normalize
ubc_cells <- rl_cca %>%
  subset(subset = species == "human") %>%
  subset(subset = broad_cell_type == "UBC") 

ubc_cells@meta.data <- ubc_cells@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(
    # sexes from human datasets
    sex = case_when(
      dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human" ~ sex,
      dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human" ~ sexes$sex[match(orig.ident, sexes$orig.ident)]
    )
  )

ubc_cells$new_clusters <- factor(ubc_cells$new_clusters, levels = unique(ubc_cells$new_clusters))
levels_index <- str_order(
  levels(ubc_cells$new_clusters),
  numeric = TRUE
)
ubc_cells$new_clusters <- fct_relevel(ubc_cells$new_clusters, levels(ubc_cells$new_clusters)[levels_index])

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_sex_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(ubc_cells, split.by = "sex", group.by = "new_clusters")
bar_plot_name <- "ubc_sex_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_sex_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

human_sex_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(ubc_cells, split.by = "sex", group.by = "new_clusters", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "proportion_ubc_sex_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_sex_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

```

![The UBC clusters don't appear to have any one cluster that is dominated by a specific sex. Except for Cluster 5 perhaps, but there aren't enough cells there to tell.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240705/ubc_sex_bar_plot.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
table(ubc_cells$sex)

   F    M 
1904 4843 

```

```{r eval=FALSE}
1904/(1904+4843)

[1] 0.2821995
```

Looking at the whole RL lineage
```{r eval=FALSE}
rl_cca@meta.data <- rl_cca@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(
    # sexes from datasets
    sex = case_when(
      dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human" ~ sex,
      dataset_name %in% c("Sepp_full_cerebellum_human", "Sepp_full_cerebellum_mouse", "Vladoiu_full_cerebellum_mouse") ~ sexes$sex[match(orig.ident, sexes$orig.ident)]
    )
  )

# Replace NA values in the 'sex' column with the string "NA"
rl_cca@meta.data$sex[is.na(rl_cca@meta.data$sex)] <- "NA"

rl_cca$sex <- factor(rl_cca$sex, levels = unique(rl_cca$sex))

rl_cca$new_clusters <- factor(rl_cca$new_clusters, levels = unique(rl_cca$new_clusters))
levels_index <- str_order(
  levels(rl_cca$new_clusters),
  numeric = TRUE
)
rl_cca$new_clusters <- fct_relevel(rl_cca$new_clusters, levels(rl_cca$new_clusters)[levels_index])

human_rl <- subset(rl_cca, subset = species == "human")
mouse_rl <- subset(rl_cca, subset = species == "mouse")


source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

human_sex_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(human_rl, split.by = "sex", group.by = "new_clusters") + ggtitle("Human")
mouse_sex_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(mouse_rl, split.by = "sex", group.by = "new_clusters") + ggtitle("Mouse")
rl_sex_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(rl_cca, split.by = "sex", group.by = "new_clusters") + ggtitle("Combined")

bar_plot_name <- "rl_sex_bar_plot.png"

plt <- human_sex_bar_plot / mouse_sex_bar_plot / rl_sex_bar_plot
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
          width = 25, height = 30, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![No cluster appears to be dominated by one sex. The sex of the Vladoiu cells could not be found so manual annotation checking the XIST expression will need to be done](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240705/rl_sex_bar_plot.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
table(rl_cca$sex)

    F     M    NA   M+F 
22744 28828 29931    40 

```
